jokes.text: They're a bunch of greedy, selfish people who have our nose.
jokes.setup: Michael is explaining why his family isn't really family
jokes.punchline: The only thing connecting them is a shared physical feature
jokes.explanation: Clever character observation that reduces family bonds to genetics while being economical and true to Michael's analytical nature

jokes.text: And Aunt Lindsay. She's not my real aunt? Not her real nose. Got a picture of her when she's 14 in a swimming cap. She looks like a falcon.
jokes.setup: George Michael asks about Lindsay not being his real aunt
jokes.punchline: Michael reveals her nose job and compares her teenage appearance to a falcon
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from nose job revelation to specific visual comparison. 'Falcon' is an inspired, memorable descriptor

jokes.text: a feat he'd accomplished by using the family's portable stairway vehicle.
jokes.setup: George Senior escaped from prison
jokes.punchline: Using the overly formal description for what's clearly a step ladder
jokes.explanation: Classic Arrested Development wordplay that makes mundane objects sound important through corporate speak

jokes.text: Actually, no, that's kind of what I thought until I heard about all that Phoenix stuff.
jokes.setup: Michael assumes family thinks they went home
jokes.punchline: George Michael's matter-of-fact correction undermines Michael's assumption
jokes.explanation: George Michael's innocent honesty deflates Michael's dramatic exit fantasy

jokes.text: Yeah, like anyone would want to 'R' her.
jokes.setup: Lucille mentions having a rape horn
jokes.punchline: Buster's inappropriate comment about his mother's attractiveness
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Buster's inappropriate honesty and weird relationship with his mother

jokes.text: Because, I mean, under her school picture, it said, 'Not pictured.'
jokes.setup: Maeby calls Ann bland
jokes.punchline: The yearbook literally didn't notice she wasn't there
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist joke about Ann's blandness taken to impossible extreme. Perfect economy and originality

jokes.text: Okay, they printed a retraction in the Spring supplement
jokes.setup: The yearbook mistake about Ann
jokes.punchline: George Michael's earnest defense makes it even funnier
jokes.explanation: George Michael's sincere response to an absurd situation amplifies the comedy

jokes.text: Yeah, they had to postpone it when that foreign exchange student parked too close to the gym.
jokes.setup: School assembly was postponed
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous reason involving post-9/11 paranoia
jokes.explanation: Clever commentary on post-9/11 school security overreach through absurd example

jokes.text: Better than part I have now-- Guy who ordered strike on Pearl Harbor.
jokes.setup: Maeby offers to play Uncle Sam
jokes.punchline: Reveals she's currently cast as a Japanese war criminal
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about inappropriate school casting choices, perfectly delivered casually

jokes.text: That's it. I'm out of here. Ta-da. I'm out of here. Problem contained. We've got the attacking animal down. I'm out of this family. Seriously.
jokes.setup: Lindsay challenges Michael about making empty threats
jokes.punchline: Montage of Michael repeatedly making the same threat
jokes.explanation: Effective montage showing Michael's pattern of empty threats, building to the current crisis

jokes.text: Ta-da. I'm out of here. Problem contained. We've got the attacking animal down.
jokes.setup: Michael storms out
jokes.punchline: Family treats his dramatic exit like handling a wild animal
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dehumanizing of Michael by the family. 'Attacking animal' is perfectly callous.

jokes.text: I'll kill her first.
jokes.setup: Michael hopes Lucille doesn't kill her replacement caregiver
jokes.punchline: Buster's matter-of-fact threat of preemptive murder
jokes.explanation: Perfect Buster moment - childlike delivery of something horrific. Amazing economy and character integration.

jokes.text: I've made a huge mistake
jokes.setup: Michael mocks Gob for always saying this phrase
jokes.punchline: References Gob's signature catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Iconic series catchphrase that became a cultural reference point

jokes.text: I've never admitted to a mistake. What would I have made a mistake about?
jokes.setup: Michael quotes Gob saying 'I've made a huge mistake'
jokes.punchline: Gob immediately denies ever saying it with complete sincerity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob moment - his complete lack of self-awareness about his own catchphrase

jokes.text: Lindsay. Instead of sleeping in twin beds, why don't you and your husband take the master bedroom?
jokes.setup: Michael is listing ways family members can be independent
jokes.punchline: Reveals Lindsay and Tobias sleep in separate beds
jokes.explanation: Establishes the dysfunctional marriage dynamic between Lindsay and Tobias

jokes.text: Now, do you want to steer? or are you too old to sit on you pop's lap and drive?
jokes.setup: Michael trying to bond with his son
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly inappropriate offer given George Michael's age
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Michael's awkward parenting. Mild cringe comedy.

jokes.text: Do you have any cookies with no preservatives?
jokes.setup: Oscar has been arrested instead of George Sr
jokes.punchline: His first concern is healthy snacks
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment establishing Oscar's hippie priorities even when in custody

jokes.text: Yes. A father figure.
jokes.setup: Lucille says George Michael needs a father figure when Oscar offers to stay
jokes.punchline: Michael's disgusted emphasis on 'figure'
jokes.explanation: Michael's dry delivery emphasizes his disdain for Oscar as a role model

jokes.text: No, I know, I now. Everybody says, 'Leave it alone, leave it alone.' Does this look contagious to you? Hey, kiddo.
jokes.setup: Barry arrives as the family lawyer
jokes.punchline: Immediately asks about his obviously infected condition
jokes.explanation: Establishes Barry's incompetence and inappropriate behavior as their lawyer

jokes.text: Oh, man, I wish you hadn't said Phoenix.
jokes.setup: Barry warns about legal consequences after Michael mentions Phoenix again
jokes.punchline: Barry's repeated regret about the Phoenix revelation
jokes.explanation: Running gag about the family's inability to keep their destination secret

jokes.text: Uh, it's not Aunt Lindsay's nose. Yes, but no. Don't-don't tell them we were going to... We say we don't need them. Oh. Yeah. Okay. The new one.
jokes.setup: Michael asks what they say about family
jokes.punchline: George Michael gives the wrong answer, then adapts to the new family motto
jokes.explanation: Nice callback showing George Michael's confusion about changing family rules

jokes.text: It comes from knowing you're toned and fit.
jokes.setup: Michael asks what Lindsay is doing
jokes.punchline: She quotes what's clearly an exercise video tagline
jokes.explanation: Shows Lindsay's shallow adoption of fitness culture catchphrases

jokes.text: I see you've found an exercise tape made for people as lazy as you are.
jokes.setup: Lindsay is exercising
jokes.punchline: Michael's cutting assessment of her minimal effort approach
jokes.explanation: Sharp sibling put-down that's both mean and accurate

jokes.text: Nice to be back in a queen. It's too much pressure. I can't do this.
jokes.setup: Lindsay and Tobias finally have the master bedroom
jokes.punchline: Immediate panic about sharing a bed
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment showing their complete sexual dysfunction

jokes.text: Well, did it work for those people? No. It never does. I mean, these people somehow delude themselves into thinking it might, but... but it might work for us.
jokes.setup: Tobias suggests an open relationship as a therapist
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts his professional advice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-contradiction showing Tobias's desperation overriding his professional knowledge

jokes.text: Well, tell him I like what I see.
jokes.setup: Lindsay encounters someone at the door looking for Gob
jokes.punchline: He's attracted to Lindsay, setting up the dating mix-up
jokes.explanation: Sets up the miscommunication that leads to Lindsay's 'date'

jokes.text: In three hours, Gob had done $45,000 in damage.
jokes.setup: Michael wonders how much damage Gob could do
jokes.punchline: Specific enormous dollar amount for minimal time
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation showing Gob's incredible capacity for expensive mistakes

jokes.text: Oh, I think it's the massage table in the break room now.
jokes.setup: Michael asks where his desk went
jokes.punchline: It's been repurposed as spa equipment
jokes.explanation: Shows the absurd office changes Gob made in just three hours

jokes.text: For the same reason you should believe that a hundred dollar bill is no more than a hundred pennies.
jokes.setup: Board asks why they should believe in Gob
jokes.punchline: Gob gives a meaningless analogy that sounds profound
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob pseudo-wisdom that's mathematically correct but completely meaningless as an argument

jokes.text: It's a gaming ship.
jokes.setup: Michael criticizes the pool table as not running a tight ship
jokes.punchline: Gob's ridiculous justification through wordplay
jokes.explanation: Gob's attempt to sound clever through bad wordplay, perfectly in character

jokes.text: Star two. At the same time? No, just the star, in the corner. Star... So if I do this like that... No, turn your hand the other way. Oh, that star.
jokes.setup: Gob tries to use the office intercom
jokes.punchline: Extended sequence of him being unable to figure out basic office equipment
jokes.explanation: Shows Gob's incompetence with basic business functions through physical comedy

jokes.text: Well, if it isn't the boy who cried Phoenix.
jokes.setup: Michael returns after saying he was leaving for Phoenix
jokes.punchline: Lucille's literary reference calling out his empty threats
jokes.explanation: Brilliant literary reference that perfectly captures Michael's pattern while showing Lucille's sharp wit

jokes.text: I want you.
jokes.setup: Michael insists he doesn't need anything
jokes.punchline: Lucille's unexpected declaration
jokes.explanation: Unexpected moment of vulnerability from Lucille, economically delivered

jokes.text: Well, I wouldn't be doing this if I didn't think that was a possibility.
jokes.setup: Realtor mentions anticipating multiples
jokes.punchline: Lindsay misunderstands real estate talk as sexual
jokes.explanation: Classic misunderstanding driving the house showing/date confusion

jokes.text: More than you'll ever know.
jokes.setup: Lucille suggests Oscar would look like her with long hair
jokes.punchline: Oscar's ominous response hinting at deeper family secrets
jokes.explanation: Clever foreshadowing that gains meaning on rewatch, perfectly mysterious

jokes.text: Yes.
jokes.setup: Reporter asks if she'd enlist her son or daughter in the Army
jokes.punchline: Lucille's immediate, unhesitating answer
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Lucille's callousness delivered with devastating economy

jokes.text: Only in color, Michael. Only in color.
jokes.setup: Michael asks if Tobias is blue
jokes.punchline: Tobias's literal interpretation while covered in blue paint
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias response - overly dramatic and literal while missing the emotional subtext

jokes.text: I'm afraid I just blue myself.
jokes.setup: Tobias explains his Blue Man Group situation
jokes.punchline: Accidental innuendo in his explanation
jokes.explanation: Iconic line that became a cultural reference. Perfect accidental innuendo delivered with complete innocence

jokes.text: There's got to be a better way to say that.
jokes.setup: Tobias says 'I blue myself'
jokes.punchline: Michael's awareness of the innuendo
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight man response that acknowledges the innuendo for the audience

jokes.text: It looks like he's dead, or he is dead? It just looks like he's dead. He's got, like, blue paint on him or something. But he's going to be fine.
jokes.setup: Family thinks Tobias is dead
jokes.punchline: Doctor's confusing explanation about blue paint
jokes.explanation: Payoff to the Blue Man Group setup, though delivery is slightly convoluted

jokes.text: It looks like he's dead. Oh, my God! Oh, little guy. The tears aren't coming. The tears just aren't coming.
jokes.setup: Doctor's unclear prognosis
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's performative grief mixed with honest admission
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lindsay moment - performative emotion mixed with brutal honesty. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: This ( bleep ), Doctor. Hey, we want this comped!
jokes.setup: Doctor's continued confusion
jokes.punchline: Lindsay treating hospital like restaurant service
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lindsay entitlement - treating medical emergency like bad restaurant service. Great escalation.

jokes.text: The tears aren't coming. The tears just aren't coming.
jokes.setup: Lucille thinks Tobias is dead
jokes.punchline: Her concern is about her own inability to cry appropriately
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille moment - even in crisis, she's focused on social appearances

jokes.text: Hey, we want this comped!
jokes.setup: Doctor explains Tobias just looks dead
jokes.punchline: Gob demands free medical service for the confusion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob response treating hospital like a restaurant, completely inappropriate

jokes.text: You made a huge mistake.
jokes.setup: George Sr escapes with evidence
jokes.punchline: Uses Gob's catchphrase while making his own huge mistake
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic use of the catchphrase by the family patriarch while escaping justice
